Roo Code (Roo-Cline): autonomous AI coding agent - full filesystem and terminal access - executes commands and modifies code - sends context to external LLM providers
Cline (Claude Dev): autonomous AI coding agent - reads writes and executes code - can run terminal commands - creates files and modifies codebase - sends context to LLM APIs
Code Runner: executes code in 40+ languages from VS Code with one click - runs arbitrary scripts with full system privileges - can execute any file type including shell scripts and binaries

Live Share: real-time collaborative editing with shared terminals - servers and debugging sessions - guests can see and edit files - run terminals and access forwarded ports - sessions joinable by anyone with link
HashiCorp Vault: browse and read secrets from Vault servers directly in VS Code - enterprise secret management infrastructure access from IDE - vault tokens accessible to extensions
Remote Control: allows remote control of VS Code via HTTP API - run commands from anywhere on the network - can be exploited for unauthorized command execution
Remote - RDP: manages and launches Remote Desktop sessions from VS Code - stored RDP credentials - can be used for lateral movement to Windows machines
Remote - SSH: opens folders on remote machines via SSH with full VS Code capabilities - installs VS Code Server on remote hosts - enables full filesystem access and command execution
Remote Development Extension Pack: bundle of Remote SSH + Dev Containers + WSL + Tunnels - all remote access capabilities in one install

Remote - Tunnels: creates secure tunnels to remote machines via Microsoft dev tunnels - enables full remote control of any machine running VS Code without SSH - bypasses corporate firewalls and network segmentation
Cloudflare Tunnel: exposes local services through Cloudflare network - bypasses corporate firewalls - local services become accessible via Cloudflare URLs without opening inbound ports
LocalTunnel: exposes local server to the internet via localtunnel.me - instant public URL for any local port - bypasses firewall without configuration
ngrok for VSCode: creates ngrok tunnels exposing local ports to the public internet - bypasses firewalls via reverse tunnels - can be used for data exfiltration or unauthorized remote access
Tailscale: connects to Tailscale mesh VPN network - access remote file systems - Tailscale Funnel can expose local services to the public internet - bypasses corporate network controls

Codeium: free AI code completion and chat - sends code context to Codeium servers - free tier with broad data collection terms
Continue: open-source AI code assistant - sends code context to LLM providers (OpenAI Anthropic Ollama) for completions and chat - codebase context shared with third-party providers
GitHub Copilot: AI code assistant - sends code context to GitHub/OpenAI servers for completions - source code transmitted to external cloud - may include proprietary code and secrets in context
GitHub Copilot Chat: AI chat assistant with codebase awareness - sends code context and questions to cloud - can access workspace files and terminal output
Cody AI (Sourcegraph): AI code assistant with full codebase context - indexes and sends repository code to Sourcegraph cloud - code search across all repositories
Supermaven: AI code completion - sends code context to Supermaven cloud for fast completions - external processing of source code
Tabnine AI: AI code completion - analyzes codebase and sends context to Tabnine cloud - source code context sent to external cloud for AI processing
Postman: API development and testing platform - requires Postman account login - syncs requests and credentials to Postman cloud - auth tokens and API keys stored in collections
Thunder Client: REST API client with auth credential storage - not open source - credentials go through closed-source code - stored API keys and tokens in collections
AWS Toolkit: full AWS integration Lambda S3 CloudWatch ECS - browse and manage AWS resources from VS Code - uses AWS credentials from ~/.aws - can deploy modify and delete cloud resources
Cloud Code: Google Cloud Platform integration - deploy to GKE Cloud Run - manage APIs and resources - uses gcloud credentials
Azure Functions: create deploy and manage Azure Functions from VS Code - deploys code to cloud - Azure credential access
Azure Resources: browse and manage Azure resources directly from VS Code - Azure subscription access from IDE - can view and manage cloud resources
Azure Storage: browse and manage Azure Blob File Queue Table storage from VS Code - access to cloud storage resources - can upload download and delete data
Docker: build manage and deploy containers from VS Code - access to Docker daemon - can run arbitrary containers with network and filesystem access
Kubernetes: manage Kubernetes clusters from VS Code - kubectl access - can deploy modify and delete workloads - access to cluster secrets and configmaps
Azure Account: Azure authentication and subscription management - stores Azure OAuth tokens - provides credential access to all Azure-related extensions
Jupyter Notebooks: execute arbitrary code in Python R Julia and more - can connect to remote Jupyter kernels - full code execution environment with network and filesystem access
PowerShell: full PowerShell execution environment in VS Code - runs scripts with system privileges - access to all Windows management APIs and .NET framework
1Password: integrates 1Password vault into VS Code - retrieve generate and inject secrets - vault access from Node.js process - secrets accessible to all extensions via VS Code API
Doppler: secret manager integration - auto-complete secret references and inject secrets into development environment - secret manager access from IDE
Infisical: secret management platform integration - inject secrets into projects - access to centralized secret store from IDE
Keeper Security: integrates Keeper vault - save retrieve and generate secrets from Keeper vault within VS Code - vault credentials accessible from VS Code process
Database Client: database client for MySQL PostgreSQL MongoDB Redis ElasticSearch - full CRUD operations - broad database access with stored credentials
Redis Client: Redis database client - browse keys execute commands monitor Redis instances - Redis often stores session tokens cache data and application secrets
MongoDB for VS Code: connect to MongoDB instances - browse collections run queries manage databases - connection strings with credentials stored in VS Code
Azure Databases (CosmosDB): manage Azure Cosmos DB and MongoDB accounts - browse databases and collections - execute queries with stored credentials
SQL Server (MSSQL): connect to SQL Server Azure SQL and Fabric SQL databases - execute queries browse schemas export data - stores connection credentials including passwords
PostgreSQL: Microsoft PostgreSQL client - execute queries browse schemas - stores connection credentials
SQLTools: database management tool - connect to MySQL PostgreSQL SQLite SQL Server - execute queries and browse schemas - stores database credentials in VS Code settings
Oracle SQL Developer: end-to-end Oracle database development - execute SQL/PLSQL browse schemas manage objects - stores Oracle database credentials
PRO Deployer: SFTP/FTP deployer with auto-upload on save - stores server credentials - deploys code to remote servers
ftp-simple: simple FTP/SFTP client - stored credentials - file transfer to arbitrary servers
SFTP (liximomo): SFTP/FTP sync with auto-upload on save - stores credentials in .vscode/sftp.json often committed to repos - abandoned by maintainer no security patches
Deploy (Reloaded): deploys workspace files to various destinations including FTP SFTP local/network paths - can transfer source code to external servers
SFTP (Natizyskunk): SFTP/FTP sync with auto-upload on save - stores SSH/FTP credentials in workspace settings - can auto-upload source code to external servers
FTP/SFTP/SSH Sync Tool: multiple file transfer protocols with credential storage and auto-sync capabilities
VS Code SFTP: edit remote Linux files directly in VS Code - direct file system access to remote hosts
HashiCorp Terraform: can provision and modify cloud infrastructure directly from VS Code - access to Terraform state files which contain secrets - cloud provider credentials required
Pulumi: infrastructure as code - create modify and destroy cloud resources from VS Code - access to cloud provider credentials (AWS Azure GCP)
Tilt: Kubernetes local development with live updates - deploys to local and remote k8s clusters - manages container builds and deployments
Coder Remote: connect to Coder cloud development environments - remote workspaces outside corporate network - full terminal and filesystem access on remote VMs
CursorRemote: remote control local Cursor AI agent from phone or Telegram - monitor approve and send tasks remotely - out-of-band control channel via Telegram bot
GitHub Codespaces: cloud-hosted development environments with full terminal and network access - code and secrets accessible from cloud VMs outside corporate network
Gitpod: cloud-hosted development environments outside corporate control - source code accessed from external cloud VMs
SSH FS: mount remote filesystems over SSH directly in VS Code workspace - persistent SSH connections - stored SSH credentials and keys
Dev Containers: opens folders inside Docker containers with full VS Code capabilities - can mount host filesystem into containers - container escape risks
WSL: opens folders in Windows Subsystem for Linux - WSL environments may have different security policies than Windows host - accesses Windows filesystem from Linux context
Remote Explorer: unified view of all remote machines accessible via SSH and Tunnels - enumerates and provides quick access to all configured remote targets
AirCodum: smartphone-based remote control for VS Code - control IDE actions and send files from phone - out-of-band remote control channel
VSCode Proxy Manager: manage proxy settings - can redirect VS Code network traffic through arbitrary proxies - may bypass corporate proxy controls

REST Client: send HTTP requests directly from VS Code - can interact with any API endpoint - auth credentials stored in .http files often committed to repos
AWS boto3: Python AWS SDK integration - autocomplete for AWS API calls - used with AWS credentials for cloud resource management
Live Server: launches local HTTP/HTTPS server exposing workspace files - serves files on a network-accessible port - can expose source code and sensitive files to the local network
GitLens: supercharged Git capabilities - full access to git history commits and diffs - can access all repository data including historical secrets committed and later removed
GitHub Pull Requests: manages PRs and issues from VS Code - GitHub OAuth token access - can create modify and merge pull requests
YAML/XML with schema validation (Red Hat): not directly risky but commonly used for Kubernetes and cloud infrastructure configuration - may expose infrastructure details
Remote - SSH: Editing Configuration Files: edits SSH configuration files directly - modifies SSH host configurations
